content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest is note/source-only in the hot path; hard claims/tasks can be produced by later consolidation. Original router reason: Imported document section provides durable design rationale and a reusable maturity framework, best stored as note-level knowledge with source linkage.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/task/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; `Claim` schema role and quality invariants in v3; In `02. Data Model` section 3.5, `Claim` is the atomic fact/relation unit for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval; each claim should represent one predicate application and remain self-contained, with explicit statements preferred over inference and weak/soft meaning redirected to `Note` instead of low-quality claims.; scope=Applies to section 3.5 `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory layer with lifecycle and linkage; `Note` is explicitly positioned between raw sources and hard claims: a single self-contained semantic fragment for retrieval-worthy context (decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) with status/retrieval/maturity lifecycle, evidence linkage, entity linkage, and note-to-note relations.; scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; v3 data model uses layered memory architecture with two cross-cutting concerns; In `v3`, the data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle` are defined as cross-cutting concerns.; scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Task entity usage rules and lifecycle invariants; In section 3.7, `Task` is operational memory for future actions and should be created only when real future action is required. Core invariants are: plain questions are not tasks, requests are not commitments, each task must represent one clear commitment, and status should be updated instead of creating duplicates; evidence linkage is handled via separate `task_sources` with `support_type` (`direct | derived_from_note | imported`).; scope=Applies to section 3.7 `Task` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; `Profile` as compact rebuildable projection layer; Section 3.6 defines `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection (not source of truth) built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in `Claim`/`Note`, and remain rebuildable.; scope=Applies to section 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; `Source` component role and invariants in v3 memory model; `Source` is the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check and explainability. It should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` separate from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit delete policy.; scope=Applies to section 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; `PredicateCatalog` defines reconciliation behavior per predicate; In the v3 memory model, `PredicateCatalog` is a configuration component (not a memory item) that controls how each predicate is reconciled, including cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and whether facts project into profile/task layers.; scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<7> DIRECTION; Episode is post-MVP optional memory for reusable lessons; Section 3.8 defines `Episode` as an optional memory type intended for later phases (not MVP) to preserve transferable experience such as successful tactics, failure patterns, and reusable lessons. Episodes must avoid raw chain-of-thought and store lesson-oriented outcomes rather than full reasoning traces.; scope=Applies to section 3.8 `Episode` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; MemoryRun recommended schema and invariants for idempotent, durable run logging; The section lists recommended `MemoryRun` fields (including `run_type`, `trigger_mode`, retrieval/prompt/model/IO/ops/status/error metrics) and states two invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety.; scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<9> DOC_DIGEST; `Entity` as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge policy; `Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify mentions and anchor claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with alias and merge support. Merge must preserve history, aliases must resolve to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges must be reversible via audit trail.; scope=Applies to section 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<10> DOC_DIGEST; MemoryRun is the audit/debug run log for pipeline observability; Section 3.9 defines `MemoryRun` as an audit trail and debug layer to track prompt/model usage, retrieved items, budget, proposed vs applied operations, and pipeline failure points for each run.; scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Claim; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Claim; reason=Existing CONCEPT entity exactly matches the mention and context.
- mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Existing CONCEPT entity exactly matches the mention and context.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=project:<0>|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|Note|Claim|Note maturity
        text_queries=Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`|different roles of Claim vs Note|factual answers reconciliation predicate rules profile updates time-aware status answers|Note maturity fresh stabilizing mature consolidated|maturity promotion signals retrieval multi-source survived without supersede recurring rationale|maturity degradation contradiction supersede scope drift long-term disuse
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f] EXTERNAL

Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim` .. 5. Note maturity
Document lines: 540-583

## 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`

Потому что у них разная работа.

### `Claim`
Нужен для:
- точных factual answers
- reconciliation через predicate rules
- profile updates
- time-aware status answers

### `Note`
Нужен для:
- richer retrieval
- decision history
- rationale
- partial or evolving meaning
- later consolidation

Если хранить все как `Claim`, получится ложная точность.
Если хранить все как `Note`, получится слишком мягкая память.

## 5. Note maturity

`Note` нельзя оставлять без модели зрелости.

Рекомендуемый паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note поддержан несколькими sources
- note пережил время без supersede
- note покрывает recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать / обнулять:
- contradiction
- supersede
- scope drift
- long-term disuse

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
